gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/358)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/358)
text: رَجُلٍ جَعَلَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ عِتْقَ رَقَبَةٍ فَأَعْتَقَ أَشَلَّ [أَوْ] أَعْرَجَ قَالَ إِذَا كَانَ مِمَّا يُبَاعُ أَجْزَأَ عَنْهُ إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ سَمَّى فَعَلَيْهِ مَا اشْتَرَطَ وَ سَمَّى .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Ahmad Bin Al Husayn, from Amro Bin Saeed, from Musadda Bin Sadaqa, from Ammar Al Sabity,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> from his<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who made it upon himself to the freeing of a neck, be he paralysed or lame. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If he was from what is being sold, it would suffice from it, except he happened to have specified it, so upon him would be what he had stipulated and specified’.
en.sarwar: 16. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ahmad ibn al-Husayn from ‘Amr ibn Sa‘id from Musaddiq ibn Sadaqah from ‘Ammar al-Sabatiy who has narrated the following: “About the case of a man who makes it upon himself to free a slave then sets free a paralyzed or lame one, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, Tf he is sellable, it is sufficient unless he has specified, in which case he must meet the condition.’”
